rubric_diff.text: Score AI Engineer candidates on the following signals:
- hard_things_done: shipped systems with real users, ambiguity resolved
· weight raised — interviewers consistently flagged scaling stories as predictive
· prefer concrete metrics (latency, RPS, cost) over adjectives
- open_source_contributions: meaningful PRs to projects > 1k stars
· counter-pattern: drive-by docs PRs do NOT count (added after Nathan eval)
· Tier-1 school = +1 to rating (over-indexed; 4 false positives in Q1)
· de-emphasized — school is a weak signal vs hard_things_done
- hackathons: weight reduced — does not predict on-the-job execution
- emotion / between-the-lines: extract hesitation in scaling questions

triggers.what: rejected after onsite — 'great hackathons but no production scaling'
marked Nathan 6.5 (was AI-scored 8.0) — drive-by OSS contributions
added counter-pattern: 'tier-1 school ≠ shipped under load'
